binaries.title: hidparse.sys - HID collection allocation path: both builds fully initialize allocated structures; only an input-range check differs (no security-relevant change)

changed_functions.name: HidP_AllocateCollections (sub_1C000D280)
key_changes: Collection node (0x30 bytes) is zero-initialized across all six qwords (offsets 0x00, 0x08, 0x10, 0x18, 0x20, 0x28) in BOTH builds before it is linked into the parent list. Node contents are fully defined in both binaries; there is no uninitialized-memory window.
The HidP KDR collection-description structure is memset to zero before any field is written in BOTH builds; its size is derived from the parsed counts (104*(...) + 16*(...) + 148).

Collection node allocation size is 0x30 bytes in both builds. Unpatched encodes it as 'mov edx, 0x30'; patched encodes it as 'lea edx, [r13+0x2f]' where r13 == 1 at that point (node is allocated only for the outermost collection). The decompiled size is 0x30 in both.

In the End-Collection (case 0xb7, HID item 0xC0) finalization path, the patched build omits the 'lea eax, [r14+rbp]; cmp eax, 0xff; ja error' range check present in the unpatched build. The count fields are byte-truncated ('movzx eax, r11b') and the output buffer is sized from the same counts in both builds, so no memory-safety property changes; the only observable effect is whether a descriptor with an oversized combined count is rejected (unpatched) or accepted (patched).
All remaining differences are register allocation and instruction scheduling from recompilation (e.g. r13/r15, rsi/r12, r14/r9 register swaps; stack frame reduced from 0x98 to 0x88).
changed_functions.security_notes: HidP_AllocateCollections (sub_1C000D280, called by the exported HidP_GetCollectionDescription) parses HID report descriptors. On a Collection item (0xA1) at the outermost level it allocates a 0x30-byte node via ExAllocatePoolWithTag('HidP'); on the matching End-Collection (0xC0) it allocates the variable-length HidP KDR descriptor. In BOTH builds the node is fully zeroed (six qword writes at offsets 0x00-0x28 via 'xor eax,eax; mov [r10+off], rax' at 0x1C000D418-0x1C000D430 unpatched / 0x1C000D410-0x1C000D428 patched) before it is linked at 'mov [rdi+0x28], r10' (0x1C000D439 unpatched / 0x1C000D431 patched). The HidP KDR structure is memset to zero before field population in both builds. Consequently no uninitialized pool memory is exposed to the caller in either build. The sole logic difference is the End-Collection path dropping an '(count_a + count_b) > 0xFF' early-error check; counts are byte-truncated and the buffer is sized from the same counts either way, so the change carries no out-of-bounds read or write. This function makes no security-relevant memory-safety change between the two builds.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
=== Collection node allocation and zeroing (both builds, success path) ===

UNPATCHED (0x1C000D3ED..0x1C000D439):
  mov     edx, 0x30                 ; fixed 0x30-byte allocation
  call    __imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
  ...
  test    rax, rax
  je      <alloc-fail>
  xor     eax, eax
  mov     [r10], rax                ; node[0x00] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x8], rax            ; node[0x08] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x10], rax           ; node[0x10] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x18], rax           ; node[0x18] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x20], rax           ; node[0x20] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x28], rax           ; node[0x28] = 0
  mov     [rdi+0x28], r10           ; link fully-zeroed node

PATCHED (0x1C000D3E0..0x1C000D431):
  lea     edx, [r13+0x2f]           ; r13 == 1 here, so 0x30-byte allocation
  call    __imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
  ...
  test    rax, rax
  je      <alloc-fail>
  xor     eax, eax
  mov     [r10], rax                ; node[0x00] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x8], rax            ; node[0x08] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x10], rax           ; node[0x10] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x18], rax           ; node[0x18] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x20], rax           ; node[0x20] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x28], rax           ; node[0x28] = 0
  mov     [rdi+0x28], r10           ; link fully-zeroed node

The node is fully zero-initialized in both builds; there is no missing-initialization difference.

=== End-Collection (case 0xb7 / HID item 0xC0) finalization: the one differing check ===

UNPATCHED (0x1C000D52C..0x1C000D552):
  lea     eax, [r14+rbp]            ; eax = count_a + count_b
  mov     r9, [r10+0x20]
  mov     dword [r9], 0x50646948    ; 'HidP'
  mov     dword [r9+0x4], 0x52444b20 ; ' KDR'
  mov     [r9+0x10], r13w
  mov     [r9+0x14], r13w
  cmp     eax, 0xff                 ; range check
  ja      0x1c000d974               ; reject if sum > 255
  movzx   eax, r11b                 ; count fields byte-truncated
  mov     [r9+0x18], ax

PATCHED (0x1C000D525..): no sum/compare; same byte-truncated writes
  mov     r11d, [rsp+0x88+var_50]
  movzx   eax, r11b
  mov     r9, [r10+0x20]
  mov     [r9+0x18], ax
  mov     dword [r9], 0x50646948    ; 'HidP'
  mov     dword [r9+0x4], 0x52444b20 ; ' KDR'

The HidP KDR structure is memset(buf, 0, size) in both builds before these writes, and offsets 0x18-0x2A fall inside the allocated size (>= 148 bytes), so the writes are in-bounds regardless of the removed check.

security_findings.entry_point: HidP_GetCollectionDescription (exported function at 0x1C000B010) — parses raw HID report descriptors provided by a HID device
call_chain: HidP_AllocateCollections (sub_1C000D280) — HID descriptor parser, case 0x98 (Collection start, HID item 0xA1) and case 0xb7 (End Collection, HID item 0xC0)
ExAllocatePoolWithTag — allocates collection node (0x30) and HidP KDR descriptor, both fully zero-initialized before use
security_findings.vulnerability_class: No security-relevant change (input-validation relaxation / recompilation). Reference KB class CWE-908 is not exhibited by the code.
security_findings.description: HidP_AllocateCollections (sub_1C000D280), called by the exported HidP_GetCollectionDescription, parses HID report descriptors. When it encounters a Collection item (0xA1) at the outermost nesting level it allocates a 0x30-byte collection node via ExAllocatePoolWithTag (tag 'HidP'); when it encounters the matching End-Collection item (0xC0) it allocates the variable-length HidP KDR descriptor structure. In BOTH the unpatched and patched builds the collection node is fully zero-initialized: all six qwords (offsets 0x00, 0x08, 0x10, 0x18, 0x20, 0x28) are written to zero via 'xor eax,eax' followed by 'mov [r10+offset], rax' before the node is linked into the parent list with 'mov [rdi+0x28], r10'. Likewise, the HidP KDR structure is cleared with memset(buf, 0, size) before any field is populated in both builds. Therefore no uninitialized kernel pool memory reaches the caller in either build. The only behavioral difference between the two builds is that the patched End-Collection path omits an '(count_a + count_b) > 0xFF' early-error check. Because the count fields are byte-truncated ('movzx eax, r11b') and the output buffer size is computed from the same counts in both builds, the removed check does not affect any memory-safety property; it only changes whether a descriptor with an oversized combined count is rejected (unpatched) or accepted and truncated (patched). This function makes no security-relevant memory-safety change.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: A HID device (physical or emulated via a mini-driver) presents a report descriptor. The Windows HID stack calls HidP_GetCollectionDescription, which calls HidP_AllocateCollections. Collection items (0xA1) allocate and fully zero collection nodes; the closing End-Collection (0xC0) allocates, zeroes (memset), and populates the HidP KDR structure. The observable difference between the builds is only whether a descriptor whose accumulated count exceeds 255 is rejected with status 0xC00000B9 (unpatched) or accepted with byte-truncated count fields (patched). No uninitialized memory is exposed and no out-of-bounds access occurs in either build.
security_findings.primitive: None. Both builds fully initialize the allocated node and descriptor structures, so there is no information-disclosure primitive. The output buffer is sized from the same counts that are byte-truncated into the fixed count fields, and all field writes are in-bounds, so there is no memory-corruption primitive. The build-to-build difference is a relaxation of an input-acceptance policy.
security_findings.assembly:
=== HidP_AllocateCollections — collection node allocation and zeroing (both builds) ===

UNPATCHED (0x1C000D3ED..0x1C000D439):
  mov     ecx, [rsp+0x98+PoolType]  ; PoolType
  mov     edx, 0x30                 ; fixed 0x30-byte allocation
  mov     r8d, 0x50646948           ; 'HidP' tag
  call    __imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
  mov     r10, rax
  test    rax, rax
  je      0x1c000d8f8               ; alloc fail
  xor     eax, eax
  mov     [r10], rax                ; node[0x00] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x8], rax            ; node[0x08] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x10], rax           ; node[0x10] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x18], rax           ; node[0x18] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x20], rax           ; node[0x20] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x28], rax           ; node[0x28] = 0
  test    rdi, rdi
  jz      short 0x1c000d446
  mov     [rdi+0x28], r10           ; link fully-zeroed node

PATCHED (0x1C000D3E0..0x1C000D431):
  mov     ecx, [rsp+0x88+PoolType]  ; PoolType
  lea     edx, [r13+0x2f]           ; r13 == 1 here -> 0x30-byte allocation
  mov     r8d, 0x50646948           ; 'HidP' tag
  call    __imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag
  mov     r10, rax
  test    rax, rax
  je      0x1c000d8bd               ; alloc fail
  xor     eax, eax
  mov     [r10], rax                ; node[0x00] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x8], rax            ; node[0x08] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x10], rax           ; node[0x10] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x18], rax           ; node[0x18] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x20], rax           ; node[0x20] = 0
  mov     [r10+0x28], rax           ; node[0x28] = 0
  test    rdi, rdi
  jz      short 0x1c000d43e
  mov     [rdi+0x28], r10           ; link fully-zeroed node

Both builds zero all six qwords of the node before linking it. The allocation size is 0x30 in both.

breakpoints.why: Entry to the HID descriptor parser. At entry: rcx=arg1 (descriptor buffer pointer), edx=arg2 (descriptor length), r8d=arg3 (POOL_TYPE), r9=arg4 (output pointer). Trace through the Collection (0xA1) and End-Collection (0xC0) handlers to observe allocation and initialization.

Start of the six-qword zeroing sequence for the newly allocated 0x30-byte collection node in the unpatched build (patched equivalent at 0x1C000D410). Single-step through the 'mov [r10+offset], rax' writes to confirm the node is fully zeroed before it is linked. Both builds execute this zeroing.

The 'cmp eax, 0xff' range check ('lea eax, [r14+rbp]' at 0x1C000D52C, 'ja 0x1c000d974' at 0x1C000D552) present only in the unpatched build. The HidP KDR structure is already memset to zero before this point, and the count fields written afterward are byte-truncated, so the check only governs whether an oversized-count descriptor is rejected. This instruction sequence is absent in the patched build.

watch: r10 (after ExAllocatePoolWithTag returns) — pointer to the newly allocated 0x30-byte collection node. In BOTH builds all six qwords at [r10+0x00..0x28] are written to zero before the node is linked.

r13 (patched) / depth counter — incremented on each Collection (0xA1), decremented on each End Collection (0xC0). The node is allocated only when it equals 1 (outermost collection), so 'lea edx, [r13+0x2f]' resolves to 0x30.
rbx — descriptor byte index; tracks the current position in the HID report descriptor.
[rcx + rbx] — current HID descriptor byte; 0xA1 = Collection, 0xC0 = End Collection.
rdi — pointer to parent collection node; its offset 0x28 is set to the new (already zeroed) node.
key_offsets: 0x1C000D3ED (unpatched: 'mov edx, 0x30') / 0x1C000D3E0 (patched: 'lea edx, [r13+0x2f]', r13==1) — collection node allocation size; 0x30 bytes in both.
0x1C000D418-0x1C000D430 (unpatched) / 0x1C000D410-0x1C000D428 (patched): six 'mov [r10+offset], rax' writes that zero the entire 0x30-byte node in BOTH builds.
0x1C000D439 (unpatched) / 0x1C000D431 (patched): 'mov [rdi+0x28], r10' — links the fully-zeroed node into the parent list.
0x1C000D4FC (unpatched) / 0x1C000D4F0 (patched): 'call __imp_ExAllocatePoolWithTag' for the HidP KDR descriptor, immediately followed by 'memset(buf, 0, size)' in both builds.
0x1C000D52C / 0x1C000D54D / 0x1C000D552 (unpatched only): 'lea eax, [r14+rbp]' / 'cmp eax, 0xff' / 'ja 0x1c000d974' — the End-Collection range check that is absent in the patched build.
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: Provide a HID device (physical USB device, or emulated via a mini-driver) with a report descriptor containing a collection, e.g. 0x05 0x01 (Usage Page Generic Desktop), 0x09 0x02 (Usage Mouse), 0xA1 0x01 (Application Collection), then End Collection 0xC0. When Windows enumerates the device, HidP_GetCollectionDescription calls HidP_AllocateCollections. To exercise the one behavioral difference, craft a descriptor whose accumulated collection/usage counts exceed 255: the unpatched build returns error status 0xC00000B9 from the End-Collection path, while the patched build accepts it with byte-truncated count fields.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In BOTH builds, inspecting the collection node at [r10] after the zeroing sequence shows all six qwords equal to zero, and the HidP KDR structure is zero-filled by memset before field population. No kernel pool residue is visible in either build. The only build-to-build behavioral difference appears when an oversized-count descriptor is supplied: the unpatched End-Collection path takes the 'ja 0x1c000d974' error branch (status 0xC00000B9), whereas the patched build continues and stores byte-truncated counts.
debugging_aids.notes: The collection node and the HidP KDR descriptor are both fully initialized (six-qword zeroing and memset respectively) in both builds, so there is no uninitialized-memory disclosure. The removed '(count_a + count_b) > 0xFF' check governs input acceptance, not memory safety: the count fields are byte-truncated and the output buffer is sized from the same counts, so all writes remain in-bounds. This function's build-to-build delta is not security-relevant.

summary: The single changed function between the two hidparse.sys builds is HidP_AllocateCollections (sub_1C000D280), the HID report-descriptor parser called by the exported HidP_GetCollectionDescription. In BOTH builds the collection node it allocates (0x30 bytes via ExAllocatePoolWithTag, tag 'HidP') is fully zero-initialized across all six qwords (offsets 0x00-0x28) before it is linked into the parent list, and the variable-length HidP KDR descriptor structure is memset to zero before its fields are populated. Consequently no uninitialized kernel pool memory reaches the caller in either build. The only behavioral difference is that the patched End-Collection (0xC0) path omits an '(count_a + count_b) > 0xFF' early-error check present in the unpatched build; because those counts are byte-truncated and the output buffer is sized from the same counts, the change affects only whether a descriptor with an oversized combined count is rejected (unpatched) or accepted with truncation (patched), with no out-of-bounds read or write. The remaining differences are register allocation and instruction scheduling from recompilation. This function makes no security-relevant memory-safety change.
